summary: Dict comprehensions construct dicts in one line: `{k: v for item in iter if cond}`. Requires colon `:` between key and value.
oneLineSummary: Dict comprehensions build dynamic dictionaries using `{k: v for item in iter}` syntax.
keyTakeaways: Requires colon between key and value.

commonErrors.cause: Using comma instead of colon between key and value

preInterviewChecklist: Demonstrate inverting a dict with dict comprehension

nextTopics.title: Exception Handling
nextTopics.whyNext: Learn robust error handling with try-except blocks.
